title: Life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians is growing, health officials warn
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Black life expectancy in Boston dropped from 77.8 years in 2013 to 76.2 years in 2024, widening the gap.
Leading causes of early death among Black Bostonians include unintentional overdose, cancer, and cardiometabolic disease, per recent report.
Boston Public Health Commission allocates $2 million to improve Black men's health, partnering with Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.

title: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet calls out Hawks over ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Luke Kornet, now with the Spurs, criticized the Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Night' promotional event.
The promotion highlights Magic City, a prominent Atlanta strip club, without acknowledging its nature.
Kornet urged the NBA to uphold respect for women and reconsider the event for March 16.

title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carlolyn Carcasi, 54, and James Carcasi, 53, of Bristol, were killed in a Swansea crash on Feb. 27.
The crash occurred at the intersection of Route 136 and Route 6, involving the couple's Subaru Ascent.
Driver Demitri Sousa, 28, allegedly responsible, had a prior incident involving a fatal shooting in Rhode Island.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Steve Kerr apologized for comments on Kristaps Porzingis' illness, admitting he shouldn't discuss medical issues beyond his expertise.
Porzingis, who now plays for the Golden State Warriors, has been limited to 18 games this season due to illness.
Porzingis revealed he has postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome, affecting his career and performance since his time with the Celtics.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are considering various trade options using draft picks and players ahead of the March 6 NHL deadline.
Key trade assets include Mason Lohrei, Andrew Peeke, and valuable draft picks like Toronto's 2026 first-round selection.
Potential departures include prospects such as Matt Poitras and veterans like Viktor Arvidsson, depending on the Bruins' strategic goals.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby trade rumors suggest a potential deal this week, with several teams, including the Patriots, monitoring his situation.
Adam Schefter and others indicate the Raiders may receive trade offers for Crosby, but a formal negotiation hasn't started.
The NFL trading period begins March 11, when teams can officially make moves after the 2025 contracts end.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sanusi Sadiq, 31, from Quincy, sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter in a 2023 St. Patrick's Day assault.
Victim Barry Whelan, 46, died of a traumatic brain injury after a confrontation with Sadiq in Downtown Boston.
Sadiq's retrial conviction followed a mistrial, where racial slurs were part of the dispute leading to the fatal assault.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons suggests Jayson Tatum may return to play against the Dallas Mavericks on Friday at TD Garden.
Jayson Tatum is ruled out for Tuesday's game against Milwaukee, recovering from achilles tendon repair.
The Celtics have not provided a timeline for Tatum's return, though ESPN reports he is cleared for full practice.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court to hear case challenging handgun restrictions for those under 21.
Case originated from Mikai Thomson's conviction for illegal handgun possession at age 20.
Current law prohibits handgun licenses for under-21s; federal law restricts sales to under 21.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney plans careful trade decisions to reinforce the team before the upcoming deadline.
Sweeney prioritizes long-term roster stability over short-term rentals despite the team's current playoff position.
The Bruins may trade draft picks for key players with long-term contracts, avoiding short-term solutions.
